===== config.toml (first 80) =====
# herdr — native Windows terminal multiplexer (preview beta).
# Managed by chezmoi; read via $env:HERDR_CONFIG_PATH (profile.d/00_env.ps1).
# Full default: `herdr --default-config`. Docs: https://herdr.dev/docs/configuration/

[update]
# Windows builds are preview-only; the installer rejects "stable" here.
channel = "preview"

[terminal]
# Match the rest of this repo (WezTerm / Windows Terminal default to pwsh).
default_shell = "pwsh"

[ui]
# "auto" draws herdr's own cursor on Windows to avoid ConPTY flicker;
# set "native" to hand the cursor to the outer terminal.
host_cursor = "auto"
```

1	# 20_aliases.ps1 — aliases and small helper functions. Modern-CLI replacements

3	
4	# --- modern replacements ---
5	if (Get-Command eza -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
6	    function ll { eza -l  --icons --git @args }
7	    function la { eza -la --icons --git @args }
8	    function lt { eza --tree --level=2 --icons @args }
9	    Remove-Item Alias:ls -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
10	    function ls { eza --icons @args }
11	}
12	if (Get-Command bat -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
13	    Remove-Item Alias:cat -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	    function cat { bat @args }
15	    if (-not $env:BAT_THEME) { $env:BAT_THEME = 'ansi' }
16	}
17	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
18	    Set-Alias -Name vim -Value nvim -Scope Global
19	    Set-Alias -Name vi  -Value nvim -Scope Global
20	}
21	if (Get-Command lazygit -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
22	    Set-Alias -Name lg -Value lazygit -Scope Global
23	}
24	
25	# --- unix muscle-memory ---
26	# `which foo` — resolve a command like the unix tool. pwsh's native equivalent
27	# is Get-Command (alias gcm); this prints just the path for executables and a
28	# short description for aliases / functions / cmdlets.
29	function which {
30	    param([Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Name)
31	    foreach ($n in $Name) {
32	        $c = Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
33	        if (-not $c) { Write-Error "which: $n not found"; continue }
34	        switch ($c.CommandType) {
35	            'Application' { $c.Source }
36	            'Alias'       { '{0}: aliased to {1}' -f $n, $c.Definition }
37	            default       { '{0}: {1}' -f $n, $c.CommandType }
38	        }
39	    }
40	}
41	
42	# unix / mac command names → Windows equivalents (muscle memory)
43	function ifconfig { ipconfig @args }
44	function open { Invoke-Item @args }          # open a file/dir in its default app
45	function pbcopy { $input | Set-Clipboard }   # pipe into it: some-cmd | pbcopy
46	function pbpaste { Get-Clipboard }
47	function reboot { Restart-Computer @args }   # unix `reboot`; add -Force to skip prompts
48	function touch {
49	    param([Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Path)
50	    foreach ($p in $Path) {
51	        if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $p) { (Get-Item -LiteralPath $p).LastWriteTime = Get-Date }
52	        else { New-Item -ItemType File -Path $p -Force | Out-Null }
53	    }
54	}
55	
56	# --- git shortcuts ---
57	function gs { git status @args }
58	function gd { git diff @args }
59	function ga { git add @args }
60	function gc { git commit @args }
61	function gp { git push @args }
62	function gl { git log --oneline --graph --decorate @args }
63	
64	# --- shell / dotfiles management ---
65	# reload the current session's profile
66	function reload { . $PROFILE }
67	# jump to the chezmoi source dir
68	function chezmoi-cd { Set-Location (chezmoi source-path) }
69	# chezmoi apply / update, then reload (twins of the unix `cas` / `cau`)
70	# cau uses --init so newly-added prompts get asked on pull (noop if none added).
71	function cas { chezmoi apply @args; . $PROFILE }
72	function cau { chezmoi update --init @args; . $PROFILE }
73	
74	# --- run-for: time-box an external command (e.g. `run-for 5 ping example.com`) ---
75	function run-for {
76	    param(
77	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$Seconds,
78	        [Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Command
79	    )
80	    $rest = if ($Command.Count -gt 1) { $Command[1..($Command.Count - 1)] } else { @() }
81	    $p = Start-Process -FilePath $Command[0] -ArgumentList $rest -PassThru -NoNewWindow
82	    if (-not $p.WaitForExit($Seconds * 1000)) {
83	        $p.Kill()
84	        Write-Warning "run-for: timed out after ${Seconds}s"
85	    }
86	}
87

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`

```text
1	# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.
2	
3	# UTF-8 console I/O. Without this, native tools that emit UTF-8 (git, winget's
4	# localized output, …) render as mojibake on legacy-codepage systems (e.g. GBK /
5	# cp936 on zh-* Windows). Guarded so a redirected/headless host can't error the
6	# rest of this fragment.
7	try {
8	    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
9	    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
10	    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
11	} catch { $null = $_ }
12	
13	# XDG-style base dirs. Several tools honor these on Windows (starship, atuin,
14	# zoxide state, our own copilot module) so we set them for a tidy $HOME.
15	if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
16	if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
17	if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
18	if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }
19	
20	$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
21	# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
22	$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'
23	# herdr reads its config here instead of %APPDATA%\herdr\config.toml (XDG-tidy).
24	$env:HERDR_CONFIG_PATH = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'herdr/config.toml'
25	
26	# yazi needs the MSYS `file.exe` (bundled with Git) for MIME detection; without
27	# it every entry errors "Cannot find `file` … Set it up per the Windows guide".
28	# scoop doesn't shim git's usr/bin, so point YAZI_FILE_ONE at file.exe directly;
29	# fall back to a system/winget Git install. First existing candidate wins.
30	if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE) {
31	    $fileOne = @(
32	        (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe'),
33	        'C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe'
34	    ) | Where-Object { Test-Path -LiteralPath $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
35	    if ($fileOne) { $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE = $fileOne }
36	}
37	
38	# uv-managed Python: `uv python install --default` drops python/python3 here.
39	# ~/.local/bin is prepended to PATH below, so they win over the Windows Store
40	# `python.exe` app-execution-alias.
41	$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'
42	
43	# Default editor
44	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }
45	
46	# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. scoop already adds its shims to
47	# the persisted user PATH, but a fresh session inside chezmoi apply may not have
48	# it yet; ~/.local/bin carries uv tools + the uv-managed python. Normalize each
49	# entry to backslashes (mixed slashes can fail Windows command resolution).
50	$UserPaths = @(
51	    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
52	    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims'),
53	    # herdr has no scoop shim; its bin junction lives here (opt-in installHerdr).
54	    (Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'Programs\Herdr\bin')
55	) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
56	    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique
57	
58	$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
59	foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
60	    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
61	}
62
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./.claude/plans/https-herdr-dev-docs-windows-beta-window-majestic-waterfall.md`

```text
1	# Add herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta)
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	`docs/rationale.md:64-83` currently asserts there is **no native Windows terminal
6	multiplexer** — tmux/zellij are Unix-only, so WezTerm is used as the closest
7	answer. [herdr](https://herdr.dev) has since shipped an **experimental Windows
8	beta**: a mouse-first, native (ConPTY-based) terminal multiplexer with panes,
9	splits, persistent sessions, and detach/attach — exactly the gap that section
10	describes.
11	
12	Goal: install herdr as an **opt-in** tool and manage its config, wiring it
13	through the repo the same way every other optional tool is wired.
14	
15	**Locked decisions (confirmed with user):**
16	1. **Gating** — a new dedicated `installHerdr` init toggle, default `false`
17	   (herdr is beta / preview-only / unsigned; opt-in only).
18	2. **Config** — the dotfiles manage herdr's `config.toml` as well as installing
19	   the binary.
20	
21	**Key facts driving the design:**
22	- herdr has **no scoop/winget/npm manifest** — it installs via an `irm | iex`
23	  bootstrap: `irm https://herdr.dev/install.ps1 | iex`. This is the same shape as
24	  this repo's own `Ensure-Scoop` (`run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:51-71`),
25	  so it needs a bespoke `Install-Herdr` helper, not the scoop/winget/npm paths.
26	- The installer is **non-interactive, user-scoped, no elevation**, idempotent
27	  (lock file, skips re-download). Windows is **preview-only** (honors
28	  `$env:HERDR_CHANNEL`). Bin junction lands at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Herdr\bin`
29	  (not `~/scoop/shims`), which it prepends to User PATH.
30	- The `install.ps1` itself writes **no** config file → no installer-vs-chezmoi
31	  drift; chezmoi can fully own the config.
32	- herdr config is TOML at `%APPDATA%\herdr\config.toml` by default, but honors
33	  **`HERDR_CONFIG_PATH`** to relocate it — a clean fit for this repo's XDG
34	  pattern (identical to `STARSHIP_CONFIG` in `00_env.ps1:20`). Notable non-default
35	  keys: `[update] channel` must be `preview` on Windows; `[terminal] default_shell`
36	  defaults to `"nu"` — this repo standardizes on `pwsh` (matches WezTerm).
37	
38	## Changes
39	
40	### 1. Install wiring — `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
41	
42	- **Header toggle manifest** (after line 16, in the `# Toggles` block): add
43	  `#   installHerdr         = {{ .installHerdr }}`.
44	- **New helper**, placed alongside the other `--- xxx ---` function sections
45	  (e.g. after the npm block, ~line 132). Models `Ensure-Scoop` (fetch script →
46	  scriptblock → invoke), guarded so it's a no-op when already present, failures
47	  via `Register-Failure`, checks the real install path (independent of PATH,
48	  which won't refresh mid-apply):
49	
50	  ```powershell
51	  # --- herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta) -------------
52	  # No scoop/winget/npm manifest — installs via herdr.dev's irm|iex bootstrap
53	  # (same shape as Ensure-Scoop). Windows is preview-only; the bin junction is
54	  # %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Herdr\bin (added to PATH in profile.d/00_env.ps1).
55	  function Install-Herdr {
56	      $herdrBin = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'Programs\Herdr\bin\herdr.exe'
57	      if ((Have herdr) -or (Test-Path -LiteralPath $herdrBin)) { Info 'herdr already installed'; return }
58	      Info 'installing herdr (preview) via herdr.dev/install.ps1'
59	      try {
60	          $env:HERDR_CHANNEL = 'preview'   # Windows builds are preview-only
61	          $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod https://herdr.dev/install.ps1))
62	          & $installer
63	          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $herdrBin)) { Register-Failure 'herdr' }
64	      } catch {
65	          Write-Warning "herdr install error: $_"
66	          Register-Failure 'herdr'
67	      }
68	  }
69	  ```
70	
71	- **Gated call** in the bottom `{{ if .installX -}}` region (mirror the
72	  `installTunnelTools` block, ~line 317):
73	
74	  ```
75	  {{ if .installHerdr -}}
76	  Install-Herdr
77	  {{ end -}}
78	  ```

86	    installHerdr         = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installHerdr" "Install herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta)" false }}
87	```

95	```
96	
97	### 4. PATH + config env — `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`
98	
99	- Add herdr's bin to the `$UserPaths` array (lines 48-51) so `herdr` resolves in
100	  interactive shells immediately; the existing `Where-Object { Test-Path }`
101	  filter makes it a clean no-op when herdr isn't installed:
102	
103	  ```powershell

105	  ```
106	
107	- Add `HERDR_CONFIG_PATH` next to the other XDG file-redirects (near line 20-22,
108	  by `STARSHIP_CONFIG`):
109	
110	  ```powershell
111	  # herdr reads its config here instead of %APPDATA%\herdr\config.toml (XDG-tidy).
112	  $env:HERDR_CONFIG_PATH = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'herdr/config.toml'
113	  ```
114	
115	### 5. Managed config — new file `dot_config/herdr/config.toml`
116	
117	Static TOML (no templating needed), deployed to `~/.config/herdr/config.toml`:
118	
119	```toml
120	# herdr — native Windows terminal multiplexer (preview beta).
121	# Managed by chezmoi; read via $env:HERDR_CONFIG_PATH (profile.d/00_env.ps1).
122	# Full default: `herdr --default-config`. Docs: https://herdr.dev/docs/configuration/
123	
124	[update]
125	# Windows builds are preview-only; the installer rejects "stable" here.
126	channel = "preview"
127	
128	[terminal]
129	# Match the rest of this repo (WezTerm / Windows Terminal default to pwsh).
130	default_shell = "pwsh"
131	
132	[ui]
133	# "auto" draws herdr's own cursor on Windows to avoid ConPTY flicker;
134	# set "native" to hand the cursor to the outer terminal.
135	host_cursor = "auto"
136	```
137	
138	### 6. Gate the config file — `.chezmoiignore`
139	
140	`.chezmoiignore` ignores `docs/**` etc. but not `.config/**`, so the file would
141	deploy for everyone. `.chezmoiignore` is rendered as a Go template, so gate it on
142	the toggle (append):
143	
144	```
145	{{ if not .installHerdr }}
146	.config/herdr/config.toml
147	{{ end }}
148	```

1	# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
2	# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
3	if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
4	    function y {
5	        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
6	        try {
7	            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
8	            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
9	            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
10	                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
11	            }
12	        } finally {
13	            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	        }
15	    }
16	}
17

 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/shell/22_sesh.sh`

```text
1	# 22_sesh.sh - sesh tmux session manager (shared backend; bash 4+ / zsh).
2	# https://github.com/joshmedeski/sesh
3	#
4	# Backend that powers the user-facing commands and their aliases:
5	#   sesh-here / shere   — plain shell session at $PWD
6	#   sesh-root / sroot   — session at the current git root (or $PWD)
7	#   sesh-code / scode   — repo-scoped `coding-agent/<repo>` layout
8	#                         (nvim 75% | agent 25%, btop monitor window)
9	#   sesh-vibe / svibe   — parametric multi-agent vibe layout
10	#                         (N agent panes + lazygit + nvim windows)
11	#   sesh-sessions       — fzf-tmux picker over `sesh list`
12	#
13	# Shell wiring (the keybinding layer is per-shell):
14	#   zsh : ZLE widget + bindkey '\es'        → dot_config/zsh/tools/22_sesh.zsh
15	#   bash: ble.sh `ble-bind -x 'M-s'`         → dot_config/bash/06_sesh.bash.tmpl
16	#
17	# Bash baseline: 4+. `svibe` uses `local -A seen` for de-dup which fails on
18	# macOS system bash 3.2 at call time (function definition still parses).
19	
20	# Skip if sesh isn't installed
21	command -v sesh >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
22	
23	# Sesh session picker (fzf-tmux + sesh list).
24	# Called as a normal function in either shell; the zsh widget and the bash
25	# ble-bind callback both just invoke this. Prompt redraw on exit is handled
26	# by whatever widget engine invoked us (zle / ble.sh) — no explicit reset.
27	function sesh-sessions() {
28	    {
29	        exec </dev/tty
30	        exec <&1
31	        local session
32	        session=$(sesh list --icons | fzf-tmux -p 80%,70% \
33	            --no-sort --ansi --border-label ' sesh ' --prompt '⚡  ' \
34	            --header '  ^a all ^t tmux ^g configs ^x zoxide ^d tmux kill ^f find' \
35	            --bind 'tab:down,btab:up' \
36	            --bind 'ctrl-a:change-prompt(⚡  )+reload(sesh list --icons)' \
37	            --bind 'ctrl-t:change-prompt(🪟  )+reload(sesh list -t --icons)' \
38	            --bind 'ctrl-g:change-prompt(⚙️  )+reload(sesh list -c --icons)' \
39	            --bind 'ctrl-x:change-prompt(📁  )+reload(sesh list -z --icons)' \
40	            --bind 'ctrl-f:change-prompt(🔎  )+reload(fd -H -d 2 -t d -E .Trash . ~)' \
41	            --bind 'ctrl-d:execute(tmux kill-session -t {2..})+change-prompt(⚡  )+reload(sesh list --icons)' \
42	            --preview-window 'right:55%' \
43	            --preview 'sesh preview {}' \
44	        )
45	        [ -z "$session" ] && return
46	        sesh connect "$session"
47	    }
48	}
49	
50	# ── Internal helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
51	#
52	# All helpers below take care to avoid the lowercase `path` / `cdpath` /
53	# `manpath` / `fpath` zsh-tied-array trap (see
54	# pitfalls/zsh-tied-array-path-shadowing.md).
55	
56	# Resolve the git repo root, or return non-zero if not in a repo.
57	function _sesh_git_root() {
58	    git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null
59	}
60	
61	# Sanitize a string for use as a tmux session name.
62	# tmux forbids `.` and `:` in session names; we also strip whitespace.
63	function _sesh_sanitize() {
64	    printf '%s\n' "${1//[.:[:space:]]/-}"
65	}
66	
67	# Switch to or attach to a tmux session named $1 (creating it at $2 if needed).
68	# Inside tmux: `switch-client`. Outside tmux: `attach-session`.
69	function _sesh_attach_or_switch() {
70	    local session="$1"
71	    if [ -n "$TMUX" ]; then
72	        tmux switch-client -t "$session"
73	    else
74	        tmux attach-session -t "$session"
75	    fi
76	}
77	
78	# Create (or no-op if exists) a detached tmux session at the given path.
79	# Returns 0 if the session already existed, 1 if newly created. (Caller uses
80	# this to decide whether to apply layout setup or just attach.)
81	function _sesh_ensure_session() {
82	    local session="$1" target="$2"
83	    if tmux has-session -t "=$session" 2>/dev/null; then
84	        return 0   # already exists
85	    fi
86	    tmux new-session -d -s "$session" -c "$target"
87	    return 1       # newly created
88	}
89	
90	# Wrap a known specstory provider in `specstory run X` so the agent picks up
91	# auto-save markdown logging. Bare CLI names that aren't specstory providers
92	# (currently: opencode — see backlog/specstory-opencode-support.md) pass
93	# through unchanged.
94	#
95	# Update the case list when specstory adds providers (track upstream:
96	# specstoryai/getspecstory#146 for opencode, similar issues for others).
97	#
98	# Args: $1=agent, $2=specstory_mode (auto|never; default auto)
99	#   auto  — wrap if known provider, raw otherwise (the historical default)
100	#   never — pass through raw regardless (CLI flag --no-specstory sets this)
101	function _sesh_wrap_agent() {
102	    local agent="$1" specstory_mode="${2:-auto}"
103	    if [ "$specstory_mode" = "never" ]; then
104	        # Even an empty agent goes raw → caller's responsibility to pick a
105	        # sensible default (we choose `claude` upstream when --no-specstory
106	        # is set, since `specstory run` with no arg defaults to claude too).
107	        printf '%s\n' "${agent:-claude}"
108	        return
109	    fi
110	    case "$agent" in
111	        ""|"specstory")
112	            # No agent specified → bare `specstory run` (default = claude).
113	            printf '%s\n' "specstory run"
114	            ;;
115	        claude|codex|cursor|droid|gemini)
116	            printf '%s\n' "specstory run $agent"
117	            ;;
118	        *)
119	            # Pass-through for non-specstory providers (opencode today, more
120	            # later as specstory adds support).
121	            printf '%s\n' "$agent"
122	            ;;
123	    esac
124	}
125	
126	# Build a wrapper command that controls what happens when the inner command
127	# exits (Ctrl+C, agent quit, crash). `--on-exit shell|kill|restart`:
128	#   shell   — print a hint, then drop into $SHELL so user can restart agent,
129	#             switch to lazygit, kill the pane manually, etc. (default)
130	#   kill    — let the pane close on exit (tmux default `remain-on-exit off`)
131	#   restart — wrap in `while true; do CMD; sleep 1; done` so the agent
132	#             auto-respawns. Use Ctrl+C twice quickly to break the loop and
133	#             land in shell.
134	#
135	# Args: $1=inner_cmd, $2=mode, $3=label_for_hint
136	# Stdout: the wrapped shell command (single string suitable for `tmux
137	# new-window … "<cmd>"`). Quoting is structured so user $SHELL is the one
138	# evaluating the inner command — we don't double-eval.
139	function _sesh_on_exit_wrap() {
140	    local inner="$1" mode="$2" label="${3:-agent}"
141	    # Ctrl+C sends SIGINT to the entire foreground process group, so the
142	    # wrapper shell itself dies before it can print the hint or exec the
143	    # login shell — the pane just vanishes. `trap '' INT` makes the wrapper
144	    # ignore SIGINT; the inner command still receives it and exits normally,
145	    # then control returns to the wrapper which prints the hint as intended.
146	    # (Tools that install their own SIGINT handler — btop, htop, less — were
147	    # the visible failure case; agent CLIs happened to handle Ctrl+C as
148	    # clean exit so this was masked for them.)
149	    local guard="trap '' INT;"
150	    case "$mode" in
151	        kill)
152	            printf '%s\n' "$inner"
153	            ;;
154	        restart)
155	            # `|| true` so a crash exit doesn't abort the loop on `set -e` shells
156	            printf '%s\n' "$guard while true; do $inner || true; echo '[$label exited — respawning in 1s; Ctrl+C twice to break]'; sleep 1; done; exec \$SHELL -l"
157	            ;;
158	        shell|*)
159	            # Default. Hint message uses tmux color codes — works in all
160	            # terminals tmux supports.
161	            printf '%s\n' "$guard $inner; printf '\\n\\033[33m[$label exited — back in shell. Re-run with: \\033[1m%s\\033[0;33m]\\033[0m\\n' \"$inner\"; exec \$SHELL -l"
162	            ;;
163	    esac
164	}
165	
166	# Connect to a sesh session for the current directory (creates it if missing).
167	# Lightweight: drops you into a plain shell at $PWD with NO startup command.
168	# For an editor-first session use `scode` (repo-aware, full layout) or just
169	# launch nvim manually inside the new session.
170	#
171	# Smart argument handling:
172	#   shere                              # plain shell at $PWD, no command
173	#   shere specstory run codex          # bare args → treated as command
174	#   shere -c "specstory run codex"     # explicit --command flag
175	#   shere -p ~/my-proj                 # explicit path, plain shell
176	#   shere -p ~/my-proj npm run dev     # explicit path + command
177	function sesh-here() {
178	    local cmd="" target=""
179	    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
180	        case "$1" in
181	            -c|--command) cmd="$2"; shift 2 ;;
182	            -p|--path)    target="$2"; shift 2 ;;
183	            -*)           echo "sesh-here: unknown flag $1" >&2; return 1 ;;
184	            *)            break ;;  # remaining args are the command
185	        esac
186	    done
187	    [ $# -gt 0 ] && [ -z "$cmd" ] && cmd="$*"
188	    target="${target:-$PWD}"
189	    if [ -n "$cmd" ]; then
190	        sesh connect --command "$cmd" "$target"
191	    else
192	        # Bypass sesh's default_session.startup_command (nvim) for a plain
193	        # shell. We create the session via tmux directly, then attach. This
194	        # also means the wildcard startup_command (project layout) is
195	        # bypassed — `shere` is intentionally lightweight; use `scode`,
196	        # `svibe`, or `sesh connect <path>` for layouts.
197	        local session
198	        session=$(_sesh_sanitize "$(basename "$target")")
199	        _sesh_ensure_session "$session" "$target"
200	        _sesh_attach_or_switch "$session"
201	    fi
202	}
203	
204	# Connect to the current git root when available, otherwise use $PWD.
205	# Same smart argument handling as sesh-here.
206	#   sroot                              # default startup_command
207	#   sroot specstory run codex          # bare args → command
208	#   sroot -c "specstory run codex"     # explicit --command flag
209	function sesh-root() {
210	    local cmd="" root
211	    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
212	        case "$1" in
213	            -c|--command) cmd="$2"; shift 2 ;;
214	            -*)           shift ;;
215	            *)            break ;;
216	        esac
217	    done
218	    [ $# -gt 0 ] && [ -z "$cmd" ] && cmd="$*"
219	    root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || root=$PWD
220	    if [ -n "$cmd" ]; then
221	        sesh connect --command "$cmd" "$root"
222	    else
223	        sesh connect "$root"
224	    fi
225	}
226	
227	alias shere='sesh-here'
228	alias sroot='sesh-root'
229	alias scode='sesh-code'
230	alias svibe='sesh-vibe'
231	
232	# ── scode: repo-aware coding-agent layout ───────────────────────────────────
233	#
234	# Open the coding-agent layout (nvim 75% | specstory-wrapped agent 25%, btop
235	# window) in a session NAMED FOR THE CURRENT REPO, so different repos don't
236	# collide on the single `coding-agent` session name.
237	#
238	# Session naming:
239	#   In repo /Volumes/Data/Program/Personal/foo  → session `coding-agent/foo`
240	#   Same repo re-invocation                     → attach (no duplicate)
241	#   Different repo                              → new session `coding-agent/<other>`
242	#
243	# Outside a git repo the function refuses with a hint to use `shere` or `svibe`
244	# (the heavy layout doesn't make sense for ad-hoc directories).
245	#
246	# Agent wrapping: known specstory providers (claude/codex/cursor/droid/gemini)
247	# are auto-wrapped in `specstory run X` for markdown auto-save. Others
248	# (opencode today) pass through raw — see _sesh_wrap_agent + backlog entry
249	# `specstory-opencode-support` for the upstream tracking.
250	#
251	# Exit behavior: `--on-exit shell` (default) drops to a shell with a hint when
252	# the agent exits, so Ctrl+C doesn't kill the pane. Use `--on-exit kill` for
253	# the old behavior, or `--on-exit restart` for an auto-respawn loop.
254	#
255	# Usage:
256	#   scode                              # current repo, default agent (specstory → claude)
257	#   scode codex                        # specstory run codex
258	#   scode opencode                     # opencode raw (not yet a specstory provider)
259	#   scode --on-exit kill claude        # Ctrl+C kills the right pane
260	#   scode --on-exit restart codex      # codex auto-respawns on crash
261	#   scode -p ~/some/other/repo         # explicit repo path
262	#   scode --no-attach                  # create session in background, don't switch
263	function sesh-code() {
264	    local target="" agent="" no_attach=0 on_exit="shell" specstory_mode="auto"
265	    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
266	        case "$1" in
267	            -p|--path)      target="$2"; shift 2 ;;
268	            -a|--agent)     agent="$2"; shift 2 ;;
269	            --on-exit)      on_exit="$2"; shift 2 ;;
270	            --no-specstory) specstory_mode="never"; shift ;;
271	            --specstory)    specstory_mode="auto"; shift ;;
272	            --no-attach)    no_attach=1; shift ;;
273	            --agents)
274	                echo "scode: --agents is svibe-only (scode is single-agent)." >&2
275	                echo "       For multi-agent layouts: svibe --agents '$2'" >&2
276	                return 1 ;;
277	            -h|--help)
278	                cat <<'EOF'
279	scode — open repo-scoped coding-agent layout
280	
281	Usage: scode [--path DIR] [--agent CLI] [--on-exit MODE] [--no-specstory]
282	             [--no-attach] [AGENT]
283	
284	Layout: window `editor` (nvim 75% | agent 25%) + window `monitor` (btop).
285	Session name: `coding-agent/<repo-basename>` (one session per repo).
286	Requires:     git repo (errors otherwise — use `shere` or `svibe`).
287	
288	Agent wrapping (auto, opt out with --no-specstory):
289	  claude / codex / cursor / droid / gemini  → `specstory run <agent>`
290	  opencode (and other unknown CLIs)         → raw passthrough
291	
292	--on-exit MODE controls what happens when ANY pane command exits/Ctrl+C
293	(applies to the agent pane, the nvim pane, and the btop monitor window):
294	  shell    (default) — drop to shell with a hint, command re-runnable
295	  kill                — let the pane/window close (tmux default)
296	  restart             — auto-respawn the command in a loop
297	
298	Examples:
299	  scode                            # current repo, default agent (specstory → claude)
300	  scode codex                      # right pane: specstory run codex
301	  scode opencode                   # right pane: opencode (raw)
302	  scode --no-specstory claude      # right pane: claude (raw, no markdown auto-save)
303	  scode --on-exit kill claude      # right pane closes when claude exits
304	  scode -p ~/work/foo              # explicit repo path
305	EOF
306	                return 0 ;;
307	            -*)             echo "scode: unknown flag $1" >&2; return 1 ;;
308	            *)              [ -z "$agent" ] && agent="$1"; shift ;;
309	        esac
310	    done
311	
312	    case "$on_exit" in
313	        shell|kill|restart) ;;
314	        *) echo "scode: --on-exit must be one of: shell, kill, restart (got: $on_exit)" >&2; return 1 ;;
315	    esac
316	
317	    # Resolve repo root
318	    local repo_root
319	    if [ -n "$target" ]; then
320	        repo_root=$(cd "$target" 2>/dev/null && _sesh_git_root)
321	    else
322	        repo_root=$(_sesh_git_root)
323	    fi
324	    if [ -z "$repo_root" ]; then
325	        echo "scode: not inside a git repo." >&2
326	        echo "       Use \`shere\` for a plain shell, or \`svibe\` for a vibe layout in any dir." >&2
327	        return 1
328	    fi
329	
330	    # Validate agent CLI exists in PATH (fail-fast). Specstory itself isn't
331	    # checked here — if the user passed a known provider but specstory is
332	    # missing, the pane will exit with specstory's error message and
333	    # --on-exit=shell will leave a shell to read it.
334	    if [ -n "$agent" ] && ! command -v "$agent" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
335	        echo "scode: agent CLI '$agent' not found in PATH." >&2
336	        return 1
337	    fi
338	
339	    local repo_name session
340	    repo_name=$(basename "$repo_root")
341	    session=$(_sesh_sanitize "coding-agent/${repo_name}")
342	
343	    # If session already exists, just attach (idempotent — solves the
344	    # cross-repo collision problem the old single `coding-agent` session had).
345	    if tmux has-session -t "=$session" 2>/dev/null; then
346	        [ "$no_attach" -eq 1 ] || _sesh_attach_or_switch "$session"
347	        return 0
348	    fi
349	
350	    # Create session + build layout directly with tmux commands (see svibe
351	    # comment block for why not tmuxp).
352	    #
353	    # Window 1 "editor": nvim (left, will be 75%) | wrapped agent (right, 25%)
354	    # Window 2 "monitor": btop (or htop / top fallback)
355	    local agent_inner agent_cmd
356	    agent_inner=$(_sesh_wrap_agent "$agent" "$specstory_mode")
357	    agent_cmd=$(_sesh_on_exit_wrap "$agent_inner" "$on_exit" "${agent:-agent}")
358	
359	    # nvim and monitor get the same on-exit treatment as the agent so that
360	    # quitting them drops to a shell with a re-launch hint instead of the
361	    # pane/window vanishing.
362	    local nvim_cmd monitor_inner monitor_cmd
363	    nvim_cmd=$(_sesh_on_exit_wrap "nvim" "$on_exit" "nvim")
364	
365	    tmux new-session -d -s "$session" -c "$repo_root" -n editor "$nvim_cmd"
366	    tmux split-window -h -t "${session}:editor" -c "$repo_root" "$agent_cmd"
367	    # main-vertical layout, then size the LEFT pane to 75% of window width
368	    tmux select-layout -t "${session}:editor" main-vertical >/dev/null
369	    tmux resize-pane -t "${session}:editor.1" -x 75% 2>/dev/null
370	
371	    # Monitor window
372	    if   command -v btop >/dev/null 2>&1; then monitor_inner=btop
373	    elif command -v htop >/dev/null 2>&1; then monitor_inner=htop
374	    else                                       monitor_inner=top
375	    fi
376	    monitor_cmd=$(_sesh_on_exit_wrap "$monitor_inner" "$on_exit" "$monitor_inner")
377	    tmux new-window -t "$session" -n monitor -c "$repo_root" "$monitor_cmd"
378	
379	    # Focus editor window, left (nvim) pane
380	    tmux select-window -t "${session}:editor"
381	    tmux select-pane -t "${session}:editor.1"
382	
383	    [ "$no_attach" -eq 1 ] || _sesh_attach_or_switch "$session"
384	}
385	
386	# ── svibe: parametric multi-agent vibe layout ───────────────────────────────
387	#
388	# A "vibe coding" session: window 1 has N agent panes (default 4), window 2
389	# is lazygit, window 3 is nvim. Built directly with tmux split/new-window
390	# commands so it stays parametric (tmuxp YAML can't express dynamic pane counts).
391	#
392	# Session naming: `vibe/<dir-basename>` — collision-safe across directories.
393	# Refuses outside a git repo (use `shere` for plain shells in arbitrary dirs).
394	#
395	# Each agent pane is wrapped in `specstory run X` (for known providers, opt
396	# out with --no-specstory) and additionally wrapped in an exit handler
397	# controlled by `--on-exit MODE` — same semantics as `scode`. Default `shell`
398	# keeps the pane open with a hint after the agent exits, so Ctrl+C is
399	# recoverable.
400	#
401	# Two modes for choosing which agents run:
402	#   1. Homogeneous (positional): `svibe 4 codex` → 4 panes all running codex
403	#   2. Heterogeneous (--agents):  `svibe --agents claude,codex,codex,opencode`
404	#                                 → 4 panes running those specific agents
405	#                                 (list LENGTH determines pane count;
406	#                                 positional N_AGENTS is rejected to avoid
407	#                                 ambiguity)
408	#
409	# Usage:
410	#   svibe                                          # 4× claude (specstory)
411	#   svibe 2                                        # 2× claude
412	#   svibe 4 codex                                  # 4× specstory run codex
413	#   svibe 3 opencode                               # 3× opencode (raw)
414	#   svibe --agents claude,codex,codex,opencode     # 4 panes, mixed
415	#   svibe --agents claude,opencode --on-exit kill  # 2 panes, mixed, hard exit
416	#   svibe --no-specstory 4 claude                  # 4× claude (raw, no md auto-save)
417	#   svibe --on-exit restart 4 codex                # auto-respawn loop
418	#   svibe -p ~/repo 4 claude                       # explicit path
419	#   svibe --no-attach 4 claude                     # create in background
420	function sesh-vibe() {
421	    local target="" no_attach=0 on_exit="shell" specstory_mode="auto"
422	    local n_agents=4 n_agents_set=0
423	    local agent_cli="claude"
424	    local agents_csv=""
425	    local min_width="${SVIBE_MIN_WIDTH:-80}"
426	    # Seconds to wait between launching each agent pane. Default 0.25s staggers
427	    # startup so DB-backed agents (opencode opens a single global SQLite DB and
428	    # runs `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, which needs a brief exclusive lock)
429	    # don't race on a simultaneous cold start. Set 0 to launch all at once.
430	    # See pitfalls/opencode-concurrent-launch-wal-pragma-fails.md.
431	    local launch_stagger="${SVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER:-0.25}"
432	    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
433	        case "$1" in
434	            -p|--path)      target="$2"; shift 2 ;;
435	            --on-exit)      on_exit="$2"; shift 2 ;;
436	            --agents)       agents_csv="$2"; shift 2 ;;
437	            --min-width)    min_width="$2"; shift 2 ;;
438	            --no-specstory) specstory_mode="never"; shift ;;
439	            --specstory)    specstory_mode="auto"; shift ;;
440	            --no-attach)    no_attach=1; shift ;;
441	            -h|--help)
442	                cat <<'EOF'
443	svibe — parametric multi-agent vibe layout
444	
445	Usage:
446	  svibe [--path DIR] [--on-exit MODE] [--no-specstory] [--no-attach]
447	        [--min-width COLS] [N_AGENTS] [AGENT_CLI]
448	  svibe [--path DIR] [--on-exit MODE] [--no-specstory] [--no-attach]
449	        [--min-width COLS] --agents A1,A2,A3[,...]
450	
451	Default: svibe           (N auto-picked from term_width ÷ min-width, clamp [1,12])
452	  window 1 "agents" — N agent panes (columns if all fit at ≥min-width, else tiled)
453	  window 2 "git"    — lazygit
454	  window 3 "edit"   — nvim
455	
456	Two modes for choosing agents:
457	  Homogeneous:    `svibe 4 codex` → 4 panes all codex
458	  Heterogeneous:  `svibe --agents claude,codex,codex,opencode`
459	                  → 4 panes (one each, in order); list length = pane count
460	
461	Session name: `vibe/<repo-basename>` (collision-safe per directory).
462	Requires:     git repo (use `shere` for plain shells elsewhere).
463	
464	Agent wrapping (auto, opt out with --no-specstory):
465	  claude / codex / cursor / droid / gemini  → `specstory run <agent>`
466	  opencode (and other unknown CLIs)         → raw passthrough
467	
468	--on-exit MODE controls per-pane behavior on Ctrl+C / clean exit
469	(applies to all agent panes, the lazygit window, and the nvim window):
470	  shell    (default) — drop to shell with a hint, command re-runnable
471	  kill                — let the pane/window close (tmux default)
472	  restart             — auto-respawn the command in a loop
473	
474	--min-width COLS sets the minimum readable per-pane width (default: value of
475	$SVIBE_MIN_WIDTH, else 80). It drives two decisions:
476	  • auto-pick N when you don't pass a count (N = term_width / min-width)
477	  • columns vs tiled in the agents window: columns when all N panes fit at
478	    ≥COLS wide side-by-side, otherwise tiled grid
479	Example on a 240-col terminal:
480	  min-width 80   → auto N=3, three even-horizontal columns
481	  min-width 120  → auto N=2, two even-horizontal columns
482	
483	$SVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER (seconds, default 0.25) delays each pane launch so
484	agents that share a global resource at cold start (e.g. opencode's single
485	SQLite DB + WAL pragma) don't race. Set 0 to launch all panes at once.
486	
487	Examples:
488	  svibe                                            # auto N × claude, width-aware layout
489	  svibe 2                                          # 2× claude
490	  svibe 4 codex                                    # 4× codex
491	  svibe 6 opencode                                 # 6× opencode (heavy)
492	  svibe --min-width 120                            # wider panes → fewer auto columns
493	  svibe --agents claude,codex,codex,opencode       # 4 panes, mixed
494	  svibe --agents claude,opencode --on-exit kill    # 2 panes, mixed
495	  svibe --no-specstory 4 claude                    # raw claude, no auto-save
496	EOF
497	                return 0 ;;
498	            -*)             echo "svibe: unknown flag $1" >&2; return 1 ;;
499	            *)              break ;;
500	        esac
501	    done
502	
503	    if ! [[ "$min_width" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || (( min_width < 1 )); then
504	        echo "svibe: --min-width must be a positive integer (got: $min_width)" >&2
505	        return 1
506	    fi
507	
508	    # Accept an integer or decimal number of seconds (e.g. 0, 0.25, 1). Used as
509	    # a `sleep` argument between pane launches.
510	    if ! [[ "$launch_stagger" =~ ^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$ ]]; then
511	        echo "svibe: SVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER must be a non-negative number of seconds (got: $launch_stagger)" >&2
512	        return 1
513	    fi
514	
515	    # Current terminal width, used for auto-picking N (when the user didn't
516	    # pass a count) and for the columns-vs-tiled cutover. $COLUMNS is
517	    # auto-maintained by both shells on SIGWINCH; tput is a fallback for
518	    # non-interactive invocation; 200 is a generous last resort.
519	    local term_width="${COLUMNS:-$(tput cols 2>/dev/null || echo 200)}"
520	
521	    # Build the agents array. Two paths:
522	    #   --agents claude,codex,…  → split csv, list length = pane count
523	    #   positional [N] [CLI]     → repeat CLI N times (N auto-picked if omitted)
524	    # Mixing both is rejected to keep semantics unambiguous.
525	    local -a agents
526	    agents=()
527	    if [ -n "$agents_csv" ]; then
528	        if [ $# -gt 0 ]; then
529	            echo "svibe: cannot combine --agents with positional N_AGENTS/AGENT_CLI." >&2
530	            echo "       Pick one. (e.g. drop the trailing args)" >&2
531	            return 1
532	        fi
533	        # POSIX-portable CSV parse: tr commas → newlines, while-read each.
534	        # Avoids zsh's `${=var}` word-splitting flag (which bash lacks) and
535	        # zsh's `read -A` vs bash's `read -a` flag-case mismatch.
536	        local tok
537	        while IFS= read -r tok; do
538	            # Trim single leading/trailing space (matches original behavior).
539	            tok="${tok## }"
540	            tok="${tok%% }"
541	            [ -n "$tok" ] && agents+=( "$tok" )
542	        done < <(printf '%s\n' "$agents_csv" | tr ',' '\n')
543	        if [ "${#agents[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
544	            echo "svibe: --agents list is empty." >&2
545	            return 1
546	        fi
547	    else
548	        # Positional: [n_agents] [agent_cli]
549	        if [ $# -gt 0 ]; then
550	            if [[ "$1" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
551	                n_agents="$1"; n_agents_set=1; shift
552	            fi
553	        fi
554	        [ $# -gt 0 ] && agent_cli="$1"
555	
556	        # Auto-pick N from terminal width when the user didn't pass one.
557	        # N = clamp(term_width / min_width, 1, 12). On a 240-col terminal
558	        # with min-width 80 that's N=3; at min-width 120, N=2.
559	        if [ "$n_agents_set" -eq 0 ]; then
560	            n_agents=$(( term_width / min_width ))
561	            (( n_agents < 1 )) && n_agents=1
562	            (( n_agents > 12 )) && n_agents=12
563	        fi
564	
565	        # Build a homogeneous array of length n_agents
566	        local k
567	        for (( k = 0; k < n_agents; k++ )); do
568	            agents+=( "$agent_cli" )
569	        done
570	    fi
571	
572	    case "$on_exit" in
573	        shell|kill|restart) ;;
574	        *) echo "svibe: --on-exit must be one of: shell, kill, restart (got: $on_exit)" >&2; return 1 ;;
575	    esac
576	
577	    if (( ${#agents[@]} < 1 || ${#agents[@]} > 12 )); then
578	        echo "svibe: agent count = ${#agents[@]} out of range [1, 12]" >&2
579	        return 1
580	    fi
581	
582	    # Resolve repo root
583	    local repo_root
584	    if [ -n "$target" ]; then
585	        repo_root=$(cd "$target" 2>/dev/null && _sesh_git_root)
586	    else
587	        repo_root=$(_sesh_git_root)
588	    fi
589	    if [ -z "$repo_root" ]; then
590	        echo "svibe: not inside a git repo." >&2
591	        echo "       Use \`shere\` for a plain shell in arbitrary directories." >&2
592	        return 1
593	    fi
594	
595	    # Validate ALL agent CLIs exist in PATH (fail-fast — we don't want to
596	    # build a 4-pane layout where one pane is dead). De-dup the list to keep
597	    # the error message readable. Requires bash 4+ for `local -A`.
598	    local -a missing
599	    missing=()
600	    local -A seen
601	    local a
602	    for a in "${agents[@]}"; do
603	        [ -n "${seen[$a]:-}" ] && continue
604	        seen[$a]=1
605	        if ! command -v "$a" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
606	            missing+=( "$a" )
607	        fi
608	    done
609	    if [ "${#missing[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
610	        echo "svibe: agent CLI(s) not found in PATH: ${missing[*]}" >&2
611	        local _avail=""
612	        for c in claude codex opencode cursor droid gemini; do
613	            command -v "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1 && _avail="$_avail$c "
614	        done
615	        echo "       Available: $_avail" >&2
616	        return 1
617	    fi
618	
619	    local repo_name session
620	    repo_name=$(basename "$repo_root")
621	    session=$(_sesh_sanitize "vibe/${repo_name}")
622	
623	    # Idempotent: existing session = just attach
624	    if tmux has-session -t "=$session" 2>/dev/null; then
625	        [ "$no_attach" -eq 1 ] || _sesh_attach_or_switch "$session"
626	        return 0
627	    fi
628	
629	    # Build wrapped command for each agent (the wrapping is pane-specific so
630	    # we can mix providers in one window).
631	    #
632	    # Helper: turn agent name → fully-wrapped tmux command string.
633	    _vibe_agent_cmd() {
634	        local inner
635	        inner=$(_sesh_wrap_agent "$1" "$specstory_mode")
636	        _sesh_on_exit_wrap "$inner" "$on_exit" "$1"
637	    }
638	
639	    # ── Build the session ───────────────────────────────────────────────
640	    # Window 1: agents — start with first pane running the first agent.
641	    # `${agents[@]:0:1}` is bash-style array slicing (0-indexed); zsh
642	    # supports the same syntax on `[@]`, sidestepping the bash-0/zsh-1
643	    # indexing fork.
644	    local first_agent="${agents[@]:0:1}"
645	    tmux new-session -d -s "$session" -c "$repo_root" -n agents \
646	        "$(_vibe_agent_cmd "$first_agent")"
647	
648	    # Pick window layout based on agent count vs what fits as readable
649	    # side-by-side columns at --min-width:
650	    #   N ≤ term_width / min_width → even-horizontal (columns)
651	    #   otherwise                  → tiled (grid — columns would be too narrow)
652	    local max_columns=$(( term_width / min_width ))
653	    (( max_columns < 1 )) && max_columns=1
654	    local svibe_layout
655	    if (( ${#agents[@]} <= max_columns )); then
656	        svibe_layout="even-horizontal"
657	    else
658	        svibe_layout="tiled"
659	    fi
660	
661	    # Add remaining panes (skip the first via array slice).
662	    for a in "${agents[@]:1}"; do
663	        # Stagger launches so simultaneously-spawned agents don't race on a
664	        # shared resource at cold start (opencode's global SQLite DB + WAL
665	        # pragma — see pitfalls/opencode-concurrent-launch-wal-pragma-fails.md).
666	        [ "$launch_stagger" != "0" ] && sleep "$launch_stagger"
667	        tmux split-window -t "${session}:agents" -c "$repo_root" \
668	            "$(_vibe_agent_cmd "$a")"
669	        # Re-balance after each split so layout stays uniform
670	        tmux select-layout -t "${session}:agents" "$svibe_layout" >/dev/null
671	    done
672	
673	    unset -f _vibe_agent_cmd
674	
675	    # Window 2: git (lazygit if present)
676	    # lazygit and nvim get the same on-exit handling as agents — quitting
677	    # them drops to shell with a re-launch hint rather than killing the window.
678	    local git_cmd edit_cmd
679	    if command -v lazygit >/dev/null 2>&1; then
680	        git_cmd=$(_sesh_on_exit_wrap "lazygit" "$on_exit" "lazygit")
681	        tmux new-window -t "$session" -n git -c "$repo_root" "$git_cmd"
682	    else
683	        # `git status; exec $SHELL` already lands in shell — no wrap needed.
684	        tmux new-window -t "$session" -n git -c "$repo_root" "git status; exec \$SHELL"
685	    fi
686	
687	    # Window 3: edit (nvim)
688	    edit_cmd=$(_sesh_on_exit_wrap "nvim" "$on_exit" "nvim")
689	    tmux new-window -t "$session" -n edit -c "$repo_root" "$edit_cmd"
690	
691	    # Focus agents window, first pane
692	    tmux select-window -t "${session}:agents"
693	    tmux select-pane -t "${session}:agents.1"
694	
695	    [ "$no_attach" -eq 1 ] || _sesh_attach_or_switch "$session"
696	}
697
```

1	#!/usr/bin/env sh
2	# ~/.config/herdr/review-mark.sh
3	# Source: dot_config/herdr/executable_review-mark.sh (managed by chezmoi)
4	#
5	# Set / clear / toggle a per-pane "review-pending" flag on a herdr pane, using
6	# herdr's per-pane custom-status metadata (`herdr pane report-metadata … --source
7	# review --custom-status …`). This is the herdr analog of tmux's
8	# `@bookmark_status` (dot_config/tmux/executable_toggle-bookmark.sh) — the
9	# problem it solves is the SAME: keep a session visibly flagged as "I still need
10	# to review this" even after the agent's own state flips to idle.
11	#
12	# Why custom-status and not agent state: it is pushed under a dedicated
13	# `--source review`, ORTHOGONAL to herdr's native agent detection. Verified: a
14	# pane can carry `custom_status:"⭐ REVIEW"` while `agent_status:"idle"` — so
15	# peeking into a done pane (which collapses it to idle) does NOT wipe the flag.
16	# Omitting --ttl-ms makes it persistent (no auto-expiry).
17	#
18	# The single source of truth for the mark IS herdr itself: `herdr pane get`
19	# surfaces the `custom_status` field, and `herdr pane list` (native JSON, NO
20	# --json flag) lets the `tv herdr-review` inbox enumerate flagged panes. No
21	# sidecar file.
22	#
23	# Usage:
24	#   review-mark.sh set    <pane_id> [glyph-text]   # default "⭐ REVIEW"
25	#   review-mark.sh clear  <pane_id>
26	#   review-mark.sh toggle <pane_id> [glyph-text]   # clear if flagged, else set
27	#
28	# Consumers: hmark/hunmark (dot_config/shell/24_herdr.sh), the prefix+m keybind
29	# (.chezmoitemplates/herdr/config.toml), and the herdr-review tv channel's
30	# focus_clear action. Keep the marker MATCH substring ("REVIEW") in sync with
31	# that channel's jq filter (dot_config/television/cable/herdr-review.toml).
32	set -eu
33	
34	SOURCE_ID='review'
35	MATCH='REVIEW'                 # substring the tv channel filters on (glyph-agnostic)
36	DEFAULT_STATUS='⭐ REVIEW'
37	
38	usage() {
39	    printf 'usage: %s set|clear|toggle <pane_id> [glyph-text]\n' "$0" >&2
40	    exit 64
41	}
42	
43	command -v herdr >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "review-mark: herdr not found" >&2; exit 1; }
44	
45	action="${1:-}"
46	pane="${2:-}"
47	status="${3:-$DEFAULT_STATUS}"
48	
49	[ -n "$action" ] || usage
50	[ -n "$pane" ] || usage
51	
52	_set() {
53	    herdr pane report-metadata "$pane" --source "$SOURCE_ID" --custom-status "$status" >/dev/null
54	    echo "review flag set on $pane"
55	}
56	
57	_clear() {
58	    herdr pane report-metadata "$pane" --source "$SOURCE_ID" --clear-custom-status >/dev/null
59	    echo "review flag cleared on $pane"
60	}
61	
62	case "$action" in
63	    set)   _set ;;
64	    clear) _clear ;;
65	    toggle)
66	        command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "review-mark: jq is required for toggle" >&2; exit 1; }
67	        current=$(herdr pane get "$pane" 2>/dev/null \
68	            | jq -r '.result.pane.custom_status // ""' 2>/dev/null || true)
69	        case "$current" in
70	            *"$MATCH"*) _clear ;;
71	            *)          _set ;;
72	        esac ;;
73	    *) usage ;;
74	esac
75

I've read both files. Here is the precise behavior of each requested function, with verbatim key lines, line numbers, and control-flow descriptions.

---

## File 1: `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/shell/22_sesh.sh`

### `_sesh_git_root` — lines 57-59

Verbatim:
```sh
function _sesh_git_root() {
    git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null
}
```

Behavior:
- Runs `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, stderr discarded.
- On success: prints the absolute repo root path to stdout, exit 0.
- Outside a repo: prints nothing, exit non-zero (git's exit code).
- It does NOT fall back to `$PWD` itself. The fallback is at the call sites. Two distinct fallback patterns exist:
  - `sesh-root` (line 219) uses `root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || root=$PWD` — falls back to `$PWD`.
  - `sesh-code`/`sesh-vibe` (lines 320-328, 584-593) call `_sesh_git_root` and, if the result is empty, print an error and `return 1` (they refuse outside a repo — no `$PWD` fallback).
- For an explicit target path, callers wrap it as `repo_root=$(cd "$target" 2>/dev/null && _sesh_git_root)` (line 320) — i.e. cd into the target first, then resolve the root from there.

For your PowerShell port: `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` returns a forward-slash path even on Windows.

### `_sesh_sanitize` — lines 63-65

Verbatim:
```sh
function _sesh_sanitize() {
    printf '%s\n' "${1//[.:[:space:]]/-}"
}
```

Behavior:
- Takes `$1`, and via bash/zsh pattern substitution `${var//pattern/repl}` replaces EVERY character matching the bracket class `[.:[:space:]]` with a single `-`.
- The character class is: literal `.` (dot), literal `:` (colon), and `[:space:]` (POSIX whitespace: space, tab, newline, CR, FF, VT).
- Each matching character is replaced individually with one `-` (not collapsed — three spaces become `---`).
- Rationale in the comment (lines 61-62): tmux forbids `.` and `:` in session names; whitespace is also stripped.
- Note: it does NOT touch `/`. Session names like `coding-agent/foo` and `vibe/foo` keep their slash (see lines 341, 621). So the sanitize only maps `.`, `:`, and whitespace → `-`.

PowerShell equivalent: `$s -replace '[.:\s]', '-'` (regex `\s` ≈ `[:space:]`).

### `_sesh_wrap_agent` — lines 101-124

Signature: `$1=agent`, `$2=specstory_mode` (`auto|never`, default `auto`). Emits the resolved command string on stdout (with trailing newline via `printf '%s\n'`).

Verbatim:
```sh
function _sesh_wrap_agent() {
    local agent="$1" specstory_mode="${2:-auto}"
    if [ "$specstory_mode" = "never" ]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${agent:-claude}"
        return
    fi
    case "$agent" in
        ""|"specstory")
            printf '%s\n' "specstory run"
            ;;
        claude|codex|cursor|droid|gemini)
            printf '%s\n' "specstory run $agent"
            ;;
        *)
            printf '%s\n' "$agent"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

Exact decision logic:
1. If `specstory_mode == "never"` (set by the `--no-specstory` CLI flag): emit the agent RAW, and if agent is empty, default it to `claude`. So output is `${agent:-claude}`. No wrapping ever. Returns early.
2. Otherwise (`auto` mode), a `case` on the agent name:
   - Empty string `""` OR literal `"specstory"` → emits exactly `specstory run` (no agent arg; specstory itself then defaults to claude).
   - One of the KNOWN PROVIDERS `claude`, `codex`, `cursor`, `droid`, `gemini` → emits `specstory run <agent>` (e.g. `specstory run codex`).
   - Anything else (the `*` catch-all — e.g. `opencode`, or any unrecognized CLI) → emits the agent name RAW, unchanged (pass-through).

So the "known providers" list is exactly: `claude`, `codex`, `cursor`, `droid`, `gemini` (plus the empty/`specstory` case → bare `specstory run`). `opencode` is deliberately NOT in the list and passes through raw (comments at lines 91-96, 118-123 note this is tracked upstream as specstoryai/getspecstory#146).

Returned command strings (verbatim forms):
- `never` mode: `claude` (when agent empty) or the raw agent name.
- `auto`, empty/`specstory`: `specstory run`
- `auto`, known provider: `specstory run claude` / `specstory run codex` / `specstory run cursor` / `specstory run droid` / `specstory run gemini`
- `auto`, unknown: `opencode` (raw), etc.

### `_sesh_on_exit_wrap` — lines 139-164

Signature: `$1=inner` (inner command string), `$2=mode` (`shell|kill|restart`), `$3=label` (default `agent`). Emits the wrapped shell command string on stdout, intended to be handed to `tmux new-window/split-window "<cmd>"`.

Verbatim (the whole function):
```sh
function _sesh_on_exit_wrap() {
    local inner="$1" mode="$2" label="${3:-agent}"
    local guard="trap '' INT;"
    case "$mode" in
        kill)
            printf '%s\n' "$inner"
            ;;
        restart)
            printf '%s\n' "$guard while true; do $inner || true; echo '[$label exited — respawning in 1s; Ctrl+C twice to break]'; sleep 1; done; exec \$SHELL -l"
            ;;
        shell|*)
            printf '%s\n' "$guard $inner; printf '\\n\\033[33m[$label exited — back in shell. Re-run with: \\033[1m%s\\033[0;33m]\\033[0m\\n' \"$inner\"; exec \$SHELL -l"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

Key shared idiom — the SIGINT guard (line 149):
```sh
local guard="trap '' INT;"
```
This is prepended to both the `restart` and `shell` modes (but NOT `kill`). Purpose (comments lines 141-148): Ctrl+C sends SIGINT to the whole foreground process group, which would kill the wrapper shell before it can print the hint or exec the login shell. `trap '' INT` makes the wrapper shell ignore SIGINT; the inner command still receives SIGINT and exits normally, then control returns to the wrapper. This mattered for tools with their own SIGINT handler (btop/htop/less).

The three emitted command strings, verbatim (with `$inner`, `$label` expanded by the emitting shell, but `\$SHELL` left literal so the USER's login shell evaluates it later):

- **kill** — emits just the inner command, no wrapper at all:
  ```
  <inner>
  ```
  The pane closes when the command exits (relies on tmux default `remain-on-exit off`). No guard, no hint, no respawn.

- **restart** — auto-respawn loop:
  ```
  trap '' INT; while true; do <inner> || true; echo '[<label> exited — respawning in 1s; Ctrl+C twice to break]'; sleep 1; done; exec $SHELL -l
  ```
  Notes: `|| true` prevents a crash exit from aborting the loop under `set -e`. Infinite `while true` loop re-running the inner command, echoing a message, sleeping 1s each iteration. The trailing `exec $SHELL -l` is only reached if the loop is somehow broken (Ctrl+C twice quickly) — then it drops to a login shell. `$SHELL` is literal (escaped as `\$SHELL` in source) so it expands at pane-runtime, not at wrap-time.

- **shell (default; also the `*` catch-all)** — run once, print re-run hint, drop to login shell:
  ```
  trap '' INT; <inner>; printf '\n\033[33m[<label> exited — back in shell. Re-run with: \033[1m%s\033[0;33m]\033[0m\n' "<inner>"; exec $SHELL -l
  ```
  Notes: runs `<inner>` once; after it exits, `printf` prints a colored hint. The ANSI codes are: `\033[33m` = yellow, `\033[1m` = bold, `\033[0;33m` = reset-to-yellow, `\033[0m` = full reset. The hint text embeds the inner command twice — once literally in the label text region and once via the `%s` printf arg `"<inner>"` (so the user sees the exact command to re-run). Then `exec $SHELL -l` replaces the process with a login shell so the pane stays alive at a usable prompt.

Important escaping detail for your port: in the source, the shell-mode string is written with double-escaped sequences (`\\n`, `\\033`, `\\033[1m`, `\\033[0;33m`, `\\033[0m`) and `\$SHELL`. After the emitting shell processes the double-quoted `printf '%s\n' "…"`, one level of backslash is removed, so the STRING THAT ACTUALLY LANDS in the tmux command is the single-escaped form shown above (`\n`, `\033[33m`, `$SHELL`, etc.). That single-escaped form is what the user's login shell then interprets. So the real runtime `printf` format string is `'\n\033[33m[... \033[1m%s\033[0;33m]\033[0m\n'`.

How the three helpers compose at call sites (for context on inputs/outputs):
- `sesh-code` (lines 356-357): `agent_inner=$(_sesh_wrap_agent "$agent" "$specstory_mode")` then `agent_cmd=$(_sesh_on_exit_wrap "$agent_inner" "$on_exit" "${agent:-agent}")`. The nvim pane and monitor (btop/htop/top) window get the SAME `_sesh_on_exit_wrap` treatment (lines 363, 376) with labels `nvim` and the monitor tool name.
- `sesh-vibe` (lines 633-637): per-agent helper `_vibe_agent_cmd` wraps agent → `_sesh_wrap_agent` → `_sesh_on_exit_wrap`, using the agent name as the label. lazygit (line 680) and nvim (line 688) also go through `_sesh_on_exit_wrap`.
- Default mode everywhere is `shell` (`on_exit="shell"` at lines 264, 421). Valid modes validated against `shell|kill|restart` (lines 312-315, 572-575).

---

## File 2: `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/herdr/executable_review-mark.sh`

This is the script behind `hmark`/`hunmark` (called from `dot_config/shell/24_herdr.sh` per the header comment at lines 28-31). It is `#!/usr/bin/env sh`, `set -eu` (line 32).

Constants (lines 34-36):
```sh
SOURCE_ID='review'
MATCH='REVIEW'                 # substring the tv channel filters on (glyph-agnostic)
DEFAULT_STATUS='⭐ REVIEW'
```

Argument parsing (lines 45-50):
```sh
action="${1:-}"
pane="${2:-}"
status="${3:-$DEFAULT_STATUS}"
```
- `$1` = action (`set`/`clear`/`toggle`), `$2` = pane_id, `$3` = optional glyph-text (defaults to `⭐ REVIEW`).
- Both action and pane are required (else `usage`, which exits 64).
- Requires `herdr` on PATH (line 43), else exits 1.

The two herdr CLI invocations, verbatim:

**Set** (`_set`, lines 52-55):
```sh
herdr pane report-metadata "$pane" --source "$SOURCE_ID" --custom-status "$status" >/dev/null
```
i.e. concretely: `herdr pane report-metadata <pane_id> --source review --custom-status '⭐ REVIEW'` (or the custom glyph-text from `$3`). stdout discarded; then prints `review flag set on <pane>`.

**Clear** (`_clear`, lines 57-60):
```sh
herdr pane report-metadata "$pane" --source "$SOURCE_ID" --clear-custom-status >/dev/null
```
i.e. `herdr pane report-metadata <pane_id> --source review --clear-custom-status`. stdout discarded; then prints `review flag cleared on <pane>`.

So `set` vs `clear` differ ONLY in the last flag: `--custom-status "<text>"` (set a value) vs `--clear-custom-status` (a valueless flag that removes it). Both use the same subcommand `herdr pane report-metadata <pane> --source review …`. Notably there is NO `--ttl-ms` flag, which (per header comment lines 15-16) makes the status persistent / non-expiring.

**Dispatch** (lines 62-74):
```sh
case "$action" in
    set)   _set ;;
    clear) _clear ;;
    toggle)
        command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "review-mark: jq is required for toggle" >&2; exit 1; }
        current=$(herdr pane get "$pane" 2>/dev/null \
            | jq -r '.result.pane.custom_status // ""' 2>/dev/null || true)
        case "$current" in
            *"$MATCH"*) _clear ;;
            *)          _set ;;
        esac ;;
    *) usage ;;
esac
```

Toggle control flow:
- Requires `jq` (exits 1 if missing).
- Reads current status via `herdr pane get "$pane"`, pipes to `jq -r '.result.pane.custom_status // ""'`. So the read-back CLI is `herdr pane get <pane_id>`, and the JSON path to the flag is `.result.pane.custom_status`.
- If the current status CONTAINS the substring `REVIEW` (`*"$MATCH"*`) → it's already flagged → `_clear`. Otherwise → `_set`.
- The match is a substring on `REVIEW` (glyph-agnostic), so any custom glyph text containing `REVIEW` counts as flagged. The header (lines 29-31) warns this `REVIEW` substring must stay in sync with the `herdr-review` tv channel's jq filter.

Additional herdr CLI facts from the header comments (lines 18-21), relevant if you port the inbox side too:
- `herdr pane get <pane>` surfaces the `custom_status` field (single source of truth — no sidecar file).
- `herdr pane list` returns native JSON (NO `--json` flag) and is what the `tv herdr-review` inbox uses to enumerate flagged panes.

Exit-code summary for the script: `usage` exits 64; missing herdr or missing jq exits 1; normal success exits 0 (via `set`/`clear`).

The documentation covers profile loading mechanics, reloading behavior, PATH configuration, and XDG standards.

Now I'm thinking about where the actual herdr commands should be documented — `docs/shell.md` focuses on mechanics rather than command reference, so the helpers might belong in `docs/tools.md` or need their own section. Since this is a new shell command surface, adding an "herdr workspace helpers" subsection to `docs/shell.md` (and its Chinese translation) would keep documentation consistent with the repo's structure. I need to define the internal helper functions that both files share — things like git-root lookup, sanitize, wrap-agent, and on-exit-wrap. Since PowerShell doesn't have a separate namespace, I'll prefix them with `_herdr_` to keep them clearly internal and avoid collisions with the repo's existing lowercase unix-style naming convention, even though PSScriptAnalyzer might flag the non-standard verb format. Invoke-Expression is excluded anyway, so that's fine. For the base64 approach, I'm encoding the PowerShell script using Unicode bytes and converting to base64, then passing it to `pwsh -NoLogo -EncodedCommand` — this keeps the command string space-free and safe to pass through the pane runner.

For shell mode, I'm wrapping the inner command (like `specstory run claude` or `nvim`) in a try-finally block that catches the exit, displays a yellow message with instructions to re-run, then drops back into an interactive PowerShell shell. The inner command gets placed directly as a command line since PowerShell will parse it correctly whether it's a single command like `claude` or a multi-word command like `specstory run claude`.

Now I'm moving into restart mode, which loops the inner command continuously with a respawn message and a 1-second delay between runs, allowing Ctrl+C to break out.

For kill mode, I'm skipping the wrapper entirely and passing the inner command directly to herdr pane run, which handles it as a single argument — so `specstory run claude` gets typed into the pane shell correctly without needing base64 encoding. The concern is how nested PowerShell invocations handle the base64 approach when the pane runs the command. Using `-NoExit` instead is cleaner — the encoded command runs and then the same pwsh process drops to an interactive prompt rather than spawning a nested one. For shell mode, I'd wrap the inner logic in a try-finally that displays a hint on exit, and for restart mode the while loop runs without `-NoExit` so it never terminates.

Now I need to handle the ratio calculation from awk into PowerShell: computing `1/($nTotal-$m+1)` and formatting it to exactly 4 decimal places. I'll use `'{0:0.0000}' -f (1.0/($nTotal-$m+1))` to avoid any locale-specific formatting issues like comma decimals on Chinese locales — making sure to use InvariantCulture to keep the output consistent.

With the new `HERDR_SESSION` env var, I can set it before spawning child processes and restore it afterward using try/finally in PowerShell, which keeps things simple since these calls are synchronous. I'll validate the session exists by parsing `herdr session list --json` when an explicit `-Session` parameter is passed. ```md
# herdr workspace helpers for PowerShell (port of `24_herdr.sh`)

## Context

The herdr **install + config** work is already merged (opt-in `installHerdr`
toggle, `Install-Herdr`, PATH/`HERDR_CONFIG_PATH` in `00_env.ps1`,
`dot_config/herdr/config.toml`). The old plan explicitly left the *shell-helper*
layer out of scope. This change fills that gap: PowerShell analogs of the POSIX
repo's `dot_config/shell/24_herdr.sh` workspace helpers, so the user gets the
same `hvibe`/`hcode`/`hhere`/`hroot`/`hmark`/`hunmark` muscle-memory on Windows.

herdr's CLI is fully scriptable and **prints JSON** ("Most commands print JSON
responses" — herdr.dev/docs/cli-reference); every flag the POSIX script relies on
(`--no-focus`, `--ratio`, `--cwd`, `--label`, `--workspace`, `--direction
right|down`, `--pane`) exists on the current CLI, plus a cleaner `HERDR_SESSION`
env var the POSIX file predates. So a faithful port is feasible — it just needs
PowerShell-native idioms instead of the POSIX/tmux ones.

**Untestable-here caveat (important):** herdr installs via `irm|iex` and is
opt-in (`installHerdr=false` in CI), and this checkout is macOS. So neither this
box nor `windows-latest` CI can exercise these functions — CI only proves the
fragment *parses*. Real behavior is validated by the user on their Windows box
(manual checklist below). The fragment self-guards on `Get-Command herdr`, so it
is a clean no-op everywhere herdr is absent (same pattern as `35_yazi.ps1`).

Scope confirmed with the user: **full port, all 6 commands.**

## What ships

One new self-guarding fragment **`dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1`**
(plain `.ps1`, no templating/`.chezmoiignore` gating — runtime-guarded like
`35_yazi.ps1`/`40_copilot.ps1`; sorts after `20_aliases`, before `28_tldr`).
Opens with `if (-not (Get-Command herdr -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { return }`.

### Private helpers (port of the shared `_sesh_*` + `_herdr_*` helpers)

| Helper (pwsh) | Ports | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| git-root | `_sesh_git_root` | `git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>$null`; empty outside a repo (callers handle fallback). |
| sanitize | `_sesh_sanitize` | `$s -replace '[.:\s]', '-'` (keeps `/`). |
| wrap-agent | `_sesh_wrap_agent` | `never`→raw (`claude` if empty); `auto`+known provider (`claude/codex/cursor/droid/gemini`)→`specstory run <a>`; else raw. **Windows guard:** if `specstory` is absent (it is — no Win CLI), always return raw. Future-proof if they later `just specstory-build`. |
| on-exit-wrap | `_sesh_on_exit_wrap` | **rewritten for pwsh** (see below). |
| ws-by-label | `_herdr_ws_by_label` | `herdr workspace list 2>$null \| ConvertFrom-Json`; `.result.workspaces \| ? label -eq $l \| select -First 1 -Expand workspace_id`. |
| tool-tab | `_herdr_tool_tab` | `herdr tab create --workspace … --cwd … --label … --no-focus` → `.result.root_pane.pane_id` → `herdr pane run`. |
| session-target | `_herdr_session_target` | **simplified**: validate explicit `-Session` via `herdr session list --json`; select it by setting `$env:HERDR_SESSION` for child calls (restored in `finally`). No `HERDR_SOCKET_PATH` juggling. |
| attach-if-outside | `_herdr_attach_if_outside` | `$env:HERDR_ENV` set → return; else `herdr` (default) or `herdr session attach <name>`. |

### On-exit wrapper — the one non-mechanical port

POSIX emits a **bash** snippet (`trap '' INT; …; exec $SHELL -l`). The pane shell
here is **pwsh** (`config.toml` `default_shell = "pwsh"`), so the wrapper is a
pwsh script. To avoid nested-quote breakage when passing a multi-statement
command through `herdr pane run`, encode it: build the wrapper string, then emit
**`pwsh -NoLogo [-NoExit] -EncodedCommand <base64>`** (base64 of UTF-16LE — no
spaces/quotes to escape). `try/finally` is the `trap '' INT` analog.

- **shell** (default): `pwsh -NoLogo -NoExit -EncodedCommand <b64>` where the
  decoded script is `try { <inner> } finally { Write-Host "<re-run hint>" -ForegroundColor Yellow }`. `-NoExit` drops to an interactive prompt after (pane stays alive).
- **restart**: `pwsh -NoLogo -EncodedCommand <b64>` of `while ($true) { <inner>; Write-Host '…respawning in 1s…' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Start-Sleep 1 }`.
- **kill**: no wrapper — `herdr pane run $pane $inner` raw (pane closes on exit).

`<inner>` is a simple command line (`claude`, `specstory run codex`, `nvim`,
`lazygit`, `btop`) placed as a bare pwsh statement — no `Invoke-Expression`.

### User-facing functions + aliases

`herdr-vibe`/`herdr-code`/`herdr-here`/`herdr-root`/`herdr-mark`/`herdr-unmark`,
aliased `hvibe`/`hcode`/`hhere`/`hroot`/`hmark`/`hunmark` via `Set-Alias`. Faithful
flag surface (`-Path`, `-Agents`, `-Session`, `--on-exit`, `--no-specstory`,
`--no-attach`, `--tab-per-agent`, `-h`), idempotent per workspace label, git-repo
required for vibe/code. Windows adaptations:

- **hvibe** even-column split ratios: `1/($nTotal-$m+1)`, formatted with
  **`InvariantCulture`** (`.ToString('0.0000', [Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture)`) so a zh-* / comma-decimal locale never emits `0,5000` to `--ratio`.
- **hcode** monitor tab: `btop` if present, else a small pwsh
  `Get-Process | Sort CPU` loop (no `htop`/`top` on Windows).
- Agents default to `claude`; fail-fast if a named agent CLI isn't on PATH
  (available on Windows: `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`).
- **hmark/hunmark**: *inlined*, not a separate script port — default pane
  `$env:HERDR_PANE_ID`; set = `herdr pane report-metadata $pane --source review --custom-status '⭐ REVIEW'`, clear = same with `--clear-custom-status`.

### Docs + skill (same commit, keeps `just docs-build --strict` green)

- New concise **"herdr workspace helpers"** subsection in `docs/shell.md` +
  its **`docs/shell.zh-TW.md`** twin (command table; no new nav entries).
- `.chezmoitemplates/dotfiles-windows-skill.md`: one line noting the
  `hvibe/hcode/hhere/hroot/hmark` commands exist (gated on herdr).

## Files to change

| # | File | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1` | **new** — the whole port (helpers + 6 funcs + aliases) |
| 2 | `docs/shell.md` + `docs/shell.zh-TW.md` | new "herdr workspace helpers" section (bilingual) |
| 3 | `.chezmoitemplates/dotfiles-windows-skill.md` | one-line mention of the herdr commands |

## Verification

Off-Windows (structural only — herdr can't run here; the Windows box is the real
behavioral gate):

1. **Parses** — `pwsh -NoProfile -c "[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile('dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1',[ref]$null,[ref]$e); if($e){$e;exit 1}"`.
2. **Lint** — `Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Path dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1 -Settings ./PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1` (Errors only).
3. **Base64 round-trip** — sanity-check the encode helper: decode a sample
   `-EncodedCommand` payload back to the expected wrapper script.
4. **Ratio locale** — confirm the InvariantCulture format emits `0.5000` (a `.`)
   under a comma-decimal culture (`[Globalization.CultureInfo]::new('de-DE')`).
5. **Deploys** — isolated `chezmoi apply --exclude=scripts` into a temp
   destination writes `.config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1`.
6. **Docs** — `just docs-build` stays green.

On the user's Windows box (post-merge, herdr installed): `hhere`, then `hcode`,
then `hvibe 2 claude` / `hvibe --agents claude,codex` — confirm workspace/tabs/
panes appear and attach works; run an agent to exit and confirm shell/kill/
restart on-exit behavior; `hmark`/`hunmark` toggle the ⭐ status on a pane.

## Out of scope (add to `TODO.md`/`backlog/` if wanted)

- `hmark` **toggle** action + a reusable `review-mark.ps1` (only needed if a
  `tv herdr-review` inbox channel or a `prefix+m` herdr keybind is later added —
  neither exists in this repo yet).
- A `herdr init powershell` cwd/shell-integration hook (still nothing to cache).

|---|---|---|

| wrap-agent | `_sesh_wrap_agent` | `never`→raw (`claude` if empty); `auto`+known provider (`claude/codex/cursor/droid/gemini`)→`specstory run <a>`; else raw. **Windows guard:** if `specstory` is absent (it is — no Win CLI), always return raw. Future-proof if they later `just specstory-build`. |
| on-exit-wrap | `_sesh_on_exit_wrap` | **rewritten for pwsh** (see below). |
| ws-by-label | `_herdr_ws_by_label` | `herdr workspace list 2>$null \| ConvertFrom-Json`; `.result.workspaces \| ? label -eq $l \| select -First 1 -Expand workspace_id`. |
| tool-tab | `_herdr_tool_tab` | `herdr tab create --workspace … --cwd … --label … --no-focus` → `.result.root_pane.pane_id` → `herdr pane run`. |
| session-target | `_herdr_session_target` | **simplified**: validate explicit `-Session` via `herdr session list --json`; select it by setting `$env:HERDR_SESSION` for child calls (restored in `finally`). No `HERDR_SOCKET_PATH` juggling. |
| attach-if-outside | `_herdr_attach_if_outside` | `$env:HERDR_ENV` set → return; else `herdr` (default) or `herdr session attach <name>`. |

### On-exit wrapper — the one non-mechanical port

POSIX emits a **bash** snippet (`trap '' INT; …; exec $SHELL -l`). The pane shell
here is **pwsh** (`config.toml` `default_shell = "pwsh"`), so the wrapper is a
pwsh script. To avoid nested-quote breakage when passing a multi-statement
command through `herdr pane run`, encode it: build the wrapper string, then emit
**`pwsh -NoLogo [-NoExit] -EncodedCommand <base64>`** (base64 of UTF-16LE — no
spaces/quotes to escape). `try/finally` is the `trap '' INT` analog.

- **shell** (default): `pwsh -NoLogo -NoExit -EncodedCommand <b64>` where the
  decoded script is `try { <inner> } finally { Write-Host "<re-run hint>" -ForegroundColor Yellow }`. `-NoExit` drops to an interactive prompt after (pane stays alive).
- **restart**: `pwsh -NoLogo -EncodedCommand <b64>` of `while ($true) { <inner>; Write-Host '…respawning in 1s…' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Start-Sleep 1 }`.
- **kill**: no wrapper — `herdr pane run $pane $inner` raw (pane closes on exit).

`<inner>` is a simple command line (`claude`, `specstory run codex`, `nvim`,
`lazygit`, `btop`) placed as a bare pwsh statement — no `Invoke-Expression`.

### User-facing functions + aliases

`herdr-vibe`/`herdr-code`/`herdr-here`/`herdr-root`/`herdr-mark`/`herdr-unmark`,
aliased `hvibe`/`hcode`/`hhere`/`hroot`/`hmark`/`hunmark` via `Set-Alias`. Faithful
flag surface (`-Path`, `-Agents`, `-Session`, `--on-exit`, `--no-specstory`,
`--no-attach`, `--tab-per-agent`, `-h`), idempotent per workspace label, git-repo
required for vibe/code. Windows adaptations:

- **hvibe** even-column split ratios: `1/($nTotal-$m+1)`, formatted with
  **`InvariantCulture`** (`.ToString('0.0000', [Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture)`) so a zh-* / comma-decimal locale never emits `0,5000` to `--ratio`.
- **hcode** monitor tab: `btop` if present, else a small pwsh
  `Get-Process | Sort CPU` loop (no `htop`/`top` on Windows).
- Agents default to `claude`; fail-fast if a named agent CLI isn't on PATH
  (available on Windows: `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`).
- **hmark/hunmark**: *inlined*, not a separate script port — default pane
  `$env:HERDR_PANE_ID`; set = `herdr pane report-metadata $pane --source review --custom-status '⭐ REVIEW'`, clear = same with `--clear-custom-status`.

### Docs + skill (same commit, keeps `just docs-build --strict` green)

- New concise **"herdr workspace helpers"** subsection in `docs/shell.md` +
  its **`docs/shell.zh-TW.md`** twin (command table; no new nav entries).
- `.chezmoitemplates/dotfiles-windows-skill.md`: one line noting the
  `hvibe/hcode/hhere/hroot/hmark` commands exist (gated on herdr).

## Files to change

| # | File | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1` | **new** — the whole port (helpers + 6 funcs + aliases) |
| 2 | `docs/shell.md` + `docs/shell.zh-TW.md` | new "herdr workspace helpers" section (bilingual) |
| 3 | `.chezmoitemplates/dotfiles-windows-skill.md` | one-line mention of the herdr commands |

## Verification
